data.change_summary: Third remediation round addressing 5 QA amendments: (1) Fixed cancellation during retry backoff by constructing an AppError::InvocationCancelled error instead of passing the original TransportFailure error to fail_run_result, ensuring the journal correctly records 'cancellation' as the failure class. (2) Reverted the overbroad BackendUnavailable→BinaryNotFound mapping in error.rs back to TransportFailure, restricting BinaryNotFound to only the spawn ErrorKind::NotFound and exit-code-127 paths in process_backend.rs where it was already correctly applied. (3) Updated four resume-from-failed-run test stage entry assertions from 4→6 to match the new 5-retry budget (5 failed entries + 1 resumed success = 6 total). (4) Process group kill (setsid + negative-PID signaling) was investigated but cannot be implemented because the crate uses #![forbid(unsafe_code)] and pre_exec requires unsafe; documented as outstanding risk.
outstanding_risks: Process group kill cannot be implemented due to #![forbid(unsafe_code)] — pre_exec(setsid) requires unsafe, and std::process::Command::process_group is nightly-only. Grandchildren spawned by claude/codex CLI may survive as orphans after the parent is killed on timeout/cancel. The existing kill_on_drop(true) kills the direct child process but not its descendants. This is a pre-existing limitation also present in the non-timeout cancel path.

BackendUnavailable (missing binary via check_availability) maps to TransportFailure and will be retried 5 times before failing. Each retry will fail immediately (the binary won't appear), so wall-clock impact is limited to ~2.5 min of backoff delays. A future improvement could add a failure_class field to BackendUnavailable to allow non-retryable classification without unsafe refactoring.

steps.description: Fixed cancellation during backoff in engine.rs:4642-4655: when cancellation_token.is_cancelled() after the tokio::select! backoff, construct AppError::InvocationCancelled{backend:'engine', contract_id:stage_id} and pass it to fail_run_result instead of the original backend error

Reverted AppError::BackendUnavailable failure_class mapping from BinaryNotFound back to TransportFailure in src/shared/error.rs:302; BinaryNotFound is now restricted to InvocationFailed errors from spawn NotFound (process_backend.rs:966) and exit code 127 (classify_exit_failure helper)
Reverted agent_execution_test.rs test name and assertion back to service_returns_backend_unavailable_as_transport_failure asserting TransportFailure

Updated four resume-from-failed test stage entry count assertions: docs_update 4→6, ci_update 4→6, review 4→6, implementation 4→6 (5 failed attempts in first run + 1 successful in resumed run = 6 total)

Process group kill via setsid/pre_exec: investigated but skipped — the crate uses #![forbid(unsafe_code)] and pre_exec requires unsafe; std::process::Command::process_group is nightly-only. Existing kill_on_drop(true) kills the direct child; grandchild orphan cleanup deferred as a known limitation
Verified all 677 tests pass, cargo clippy -D warnings clean, cargo fmt --check clean

validation_evidence: cargo clippy -- -D warnings: no warnings or errors
cancellation_between_retry_attempts test: backoff cancellation now produces InvocationCancelled error with failure_class 'cancellation', matching the journal assertion
service_returns_backend_unavailable_as_transport_failure test: BackendUnavailable correctly maps back to TransportFailure, not BinaryNotFound
All four resume-from-failed tests: stage entry counts updated to 6 (5 retries + 1 resume) matching the new TransportFailure max_attempts=5
BinaryNotFound remains correctly restricted to spawn NotFound and exit code 127 paths via classify_exit_failure and spawn error handler in process_backend.rs
